mission.title: metal3-io: Publish release artifacts with manifests
mission.description: Publish release artifacts with manifests. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify metal3-io troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your metal3-io deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io -n metal3-io --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review metal3-io configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant metal3-io configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io
kubectl get configmap -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=metal3-io
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**

As seen in https://github.com/metal3-io/metal3-docs/pull/414 we are not setting the image tag in the

steps.title: Apply the fix for Publish release artifacts with manifests
steps.description:
✨ 

This PR solves image tags, by adding a verification check and also setting the tag automatically 

**Which issue(s) this PR fixes

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Publish release artifacts with manifests is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io -n metal3-io --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n metal3-io --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
✨ 

This PR solves image tags, by adding a verification check and also setting the tag automatically 

**Which issue(s) this PR fixes

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with metal3-io installed or the issue environment reproducible.
